title: 5 Bruins takeaways from Don Sweeney’s pre-draft press conference
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney expresses interest in trades to address roster needs ahead of the NHL Draft.
Sweeney discusses potential selling of assets like Pavel Zacha, exploring younger player growth opportunities.
Negotiations ongoing with free agents Viktor Arvidsson and Andrew Peeke, with interest from both sides.

title: ‘Unethical’: Jaylen Brown slams ESPN, Stephen A. Smith in latest exchange of barbs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized ESPN and Stephen A. Smith for reporting his 'favorite season' comments as unethical.
Brown's longstanding feud with Smith began in 2024 over marketability rumors affecting his NBA image.
Amid trade rumors and Olympic team snub, Brown challenges Smith's recent comments on Twitch, sparking media debate.

title: Review and setlist: The Strokes’ appeal isn’t ‘hard to explain’ even 20 years later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Strokes performed at TD Garden in Boston, their first appearance there in nearly two decades, on June 23, 2026.
The band is on the 'Reality Awaits' tour promoting their upcoming seventh album, sans founding guitarist Nick Valensi.
The concert featured new singles 'Going Shopping' and 'Falling Out of Love', as well as classics like 'Someday' and 'Hard to Explain.'

title: Davio’s Northern Italian Steakhouse to close Chestnut Hill restaurant
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Davio’s Northern Italian Steakhouse in Chestnut Hill will close on June 28 due to a change in property ownership.
Owner Steve DiFillippo stated the closure is because of lease issues following the Showcase SuperLux property's ownership change.
Davio’s restaurant group has experienced several closures, but its flagship location remains open in Boston's Back Bay.

title: National group alleges ‘anti-Muslim hate campaign’ in Newton
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Council on American-Islamic Relations alleges an anti-Muslim campaign over an Eid al-Adha celebration at Newton City Hall.
A petition circulated before the event accused Muslim community partners of having terrorist ties, says CAIR-MA.
Newton Mayor Marc Laredo supports all religious celebrations, stating the city does not discriminate in public space rentals.

title: Boston Dynamics seeks to double local headcount with Greater Boston expansion
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Boston Dynamics will expand its Waltham facility to a 300,000-square-foot center, aiming to double its workforce by 2033.
The company plans to invest $100 million in the expansion, creating 1,250 jobs and consolidating operations at 1601 Trapelo Road.
The Massachusetts government awarded Boston Dynamics $25 million to support the project, highlighting the state's leadership in robotics.

title: 9-year-old seriously injured after falling from window in Hanson
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 9-year-old boy was seriously injured after falling from a third-story window in Hanson on Wednesday.
Emergency responders transported the boy to a Boston trauma center via helicopter after initial treatment.
Hanson police are investigating the fall; the boy's identity and circumstances of the incident remain undisclosed.

title: Teso Life is opening a 20,000-square-foot Downtown Crossing store Friday
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Teso Life to open a 20,000-square-foot store at 459 Washington St. on June 26.
The new store in Downtown Crossing will sell Asian snacks, beauty, toys, and home goods.
Teso Life's first New England store replaces the former Forever 21 space closed in 2021.

title: Your next Uber or Lyft ride in Boston could be a taxi
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Boston launches a one-year pilot program allowing taxis to accept Uber and Lyft rides.
The program aims to increase transportation options and enhance earnings for taxi drivers.
Taxis will charge the ride-hail app's upfront price, not the city's regulated meter fare.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the England-Ghana World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Ghana and England played to a scoreless draw at 'Boston Stadium' in Foxborough, attended by over 64,000 fans.
Ghanaian fans were particularly energetic, with extensive support also evident from the England fanbase at the match.
Jerome Opoku and Declan Rice stood out for their respective teams, while Harry Kane missed a key scoring opportunity.
